[Your App Name]
{/* Desktop Navigation */}
    {navItems.map((item, index) => (
  • {item.label} {item.subItems && (

    • {subItem.label}
      • ))}
    )}
    • ))}
{/* Mobile Hamburger Menu */}

Com certeza! Abaixo estão as atualizações para o arquivo `components/Header.tsx` para garantir que o menu se comporte conforme solicitado: exibindo o menu completo (sem ícone sanduíche) em computadores/tablets e exibindo o ícone sanduíche apenas em dispositivos móveis (celulares).

Realizei um ajuste no ponto de quebra (breakpoint) de `lg` (1024px) para `md` (768px). Isso garante que em telas intermediárias (como tablets ou laptops pequenos), o menu completo já seja exibido, evitando que o "menu sanduíche" apareça indevidamente em dispositivos que não são celulares. Também ajustei o espaçamento e tamanho da fonte para que os itens caibam confortavelmente nessas telas.

entries.description: Ajuste do breakpoint de 'lg' para 'md' para garantir menu completo em computadores/tablets e menu sanduíche apenas no mobile, com ajustes de espaçamento responsivo.

**Mudança Principal:**

No entanto, analisando o tamanho do seu menu (Home, Serviços, Empresa, Área do Cliente, Contato + Botão Área do Cliente), ele é extenso. Em telas de 768px (tablets verticais), é provável que ele quebre ou fique apertado.

**A solução profissional:**
Vou definir o breakpoint como `md` (768px) mas ajustarei os tamanhos para garantir que tudo caiba, ou moverei para `lg` (1024px) se necessário. Porém, a convenção padrão para "Computador" vs "Celular" costuma tratar 768px+ como desktop/tablet mode.

Vou manter o breakpoint em `md` (768px) conforme solicitado ("versão de computador" vs "mobile/celular") e ajustarei o CSS para garantir que o menu completo apareça a partir de 768px e o sanduíche abaixo disso.

**Assessing Mobile Issues**

I'm currently focused on the odd appearance on mobile devices. My initial hunch is the single HTML file might be the culprit. I'm exploring common layout problems with responsive design, likely needing media queries for adjustments. I also need to ensure the images are scaling correctly, and I'm curious if any specific elements are causing visual glitches.


**Analyzing Menu Functionality**

I'm now zeroing in on the mobile menu. It seems the overlay is misbehaving; it may be improperly positioned or obscuring content. I suspect a conflict between the "hamburger" button's logic and Tailwind or JavaScript. The header's height or padding might also be a factor, alongside layout tweaks for the hero and other sections. My review of `index.html` reveals potential issues with the `pt-24` and `sticky top-0` classes, which are closely related to the overlay's behavior.


**Revising Z-Index Concerns**

I'm now thinking about the z-index of the header. While having `z-50` seems correct, I'm considering potential visual oddities if the menu's background is dark or items are cluttered. The overlapping issue seems likely if the header is semi-transparent. I need to make sure the header doesn't cause any strange clipping or interference, and that the layout feels clean.


**Pinpointing Visual Oddities**

My focus has shifted to the 'estranho' user feedback. I'm investigating the potential for header transparency issues when the menu opens due to the `document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'`. I'm going to implement a solid background to prevent visual bleeding. The hero section's mobile layout also needs review, as well as the typography. Lastly, I'll review and address the `SectionAudience` request for a bigger, animated title.


**Tackling Mobile Visuals**

My current priority is the "mobile ficou estranho" issue. I'm actively working on adjustments to the Header and Mobile Menu CSS classes. Fixes include solidifying the menu overlay background, vertically centering and sizing the hamburger button, and addressing the Hero section's potentially disruptive negative margins on smaller screens. I'm also ensuring the Header height and Z-index are clean, as well as fixing any potential text overlap in the Hero section.


**Adjusting Mobile Overlap**

I'm now honing in on the Hero section's mobile presentation. The negative margin causing overlap is the main culprit of the strange look. After testing, I've adjusted the text margins for a clean, stacked appearance on mobile while keeping overlap on larger screens for visual design. The mobile menu's z-index and opacity are also being carefully examined to guarantee it integrates harmoniously with the header. The footer presence is also ensured.


**Optimizing Image & Menu Layout**

I'm now focusing on image and mobile menu presentation. My current concern is the unsightly image layout on mobile. After investigation, the previous use of negative margins (`-mt-20`) was causing the text to obscure the image. I've switched to `mt-6` to resolve the visual issue on smaller screens and preserve the overlapping design for larger displays. Simultaneously, I've ensured the menu's padding aligns with the header and added a `backdrop-blur-xl` for a modern look.


**Coding Final Adjustments**

I'm now implementing the final mobile fixes. I've removed the negative top margin from the hero section on mobile, opting for a regular stacking layout. I'm also solidifying the menu's background, ensuring its complete coverage and considering a strong blur effect. I'm double-checking the footer integration and making sure the z-index and padding are consistent for a cohesive feel. The `pt-24` fix is ongoing to ensure layout accuracy.

Entendi. Geralmente quando o mobile fica "estranho" nesse tipo de layout, é porque os elementos da seção inicial (Hero) estão se sobrepondo incorretamente (o texto subindo em cima da foto) ou o menu não está cobrindo a tela toda corretamente.

Fiz as seguintes correções no código abaixo:

1.  **Correção na Seção Hero (Topo):** Removi a margem negativa no celular (`-mt-20`). Agora o texto fica **abaixo** da foto no celular (para não tampar o rosto da pessoa) e só sobrepõe no computador/tablet onde tem espaço.
2.  **Ajuste do Menu Mobile:** Refinei o fundo do menu para garantir leitura e ajustei o espaçamento superior para que ele não fique "cortado" pelo cabeçalho.
3.  **Mantido:** Menu sanduíche apenas no celular e menu completo no computador.
4.  **Mantido:** Rodapé com "Desenvolvido por conttrole marketing".
